entities.name: SEED VOUCHERS
entities.description: A system where farmers receive vouchers to exchange for seeds at seed fairs instead of receiving free seeds

entities.name: SEED FAIRS
entities.description: Specially organized events where commercial seed firms, local producers, and traders provide seeds for exchange

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL RECOVERY
entities.description: The process of restoring agricultural productivity and seed access after a disaster

entities.name: DISASTER
entities.description: Emergency situations that disrupt agricultural production and necessitate seed distribution

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where the seed voucher and seed fair approach is described and applied

entities.name: POST-EMERGENCY SEED DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The overall approach of distributing seeds to farmers following a disaster

entities.name: SEED SYSTEMS
entities.description: The components and networks involved in the production and distribution of seeds

entities.name: SOUTHERN SUDAN
entities.description: A specific region in Africa used as a source of examples for the seed voucher approach

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: The agricultural plants and varieties that farmers select during seed fairs
